intent: Write a piece on 'how /f/movies and Wikipedia disagree about a film's legacy'. You MUST research BOTH sources: (1) Reddit — browse /f/movies AND /f/showerthoughts for posts about a specific film, franchise, or director (you pick one concrete subject). Collect at least 4 posts with title, score, comment count, and the prevailing opinion. (2) Wikipedia — open the article on the film or filmmaker and extract 3-4 facts on production, reception, and critical legacy. The report must: (a) name the subject and summarize the Wikipedia entry, (b) present the community's rolling opinion from Reddit, (c) analyze where fandom memory diverges from documented critical history, (d) conclude with a one-paragraph fair assessment. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Reddit (movies + showerthoughts) + Wikipedia. Memory-vs-record divergence analysis.
